title: Boat Rental in Amsterdam: Costs, Seasons & Tips for 2026
html:
Renting a boat in Amsterdam lets you explore its historic canals and the IJsselmeer from the water. Depart from Amsterdam Marina or Sixhaven, with prices from EUR 150 for a small motorboat half-day. BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Amsterdam or list your own.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Amsterdam?

Prices range from EUR 110 for a shared tour to EUR 1,500 for a crewed yacht per day in 2026. A small motorboat half-day costs about EUR 150, while a full-day catamaran might reach EUR 800. For the most accurate rates, you can browse boats for your dates on the BoatHire24 platform. You can browse boats for your dates in a few minutes.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Amsterdam?

Most small motorboats (up to 15 hp) do not require a licence on Amsterdam canals. For larger vessels or sailing on the IJsselmeer, a licence or a skippered option is often mandatory. The operator confirms exact requirements before booking. One option worth a look is this motor yacht.

Where to set off in Amsterdam

Several marinas offer direct access to the city’s waterways. Amsterdam Marina in NDSM-werf is a popular departure point, with facilities and parking. Sixhaven, close to Centraal Station, provides easy canal access. For the IJsselmeer, consider IJmuiden harbour or the port of Muiden. Nearby towns such as Volendam and Marken are reachable within a few hours. The island of Pampus, 5 nautical miles from Amsterdam, makes a scenic destination. You can also see more at browse the listings.

Which boat to rent

  1. Open motorboat, best for couples or small groups exploring canals; 4 guests; from EUR 150 half-day.
  2. Sloop, ideal for up to 8 friends with drinks; 8 guests; from EUR 250 half-day.
  3. Motor yacht, such as an Azimut or the Salty Sun Charters, perfect for a day on the IJsselmeer; 10 guests; from EUR 950 full-day. Book this motor yacht via the BoatHire24 listing.
  4. Catamaran, stable and spacious for parties; 12 guests; from EUR 800 full-day.
  5. Crewed yacht, includes skipper and crew, suitable for corporate events; 20 guests; from EUR 1,500 full-day.

Costs of boat rental in Amsterdam

  • Shared canal tour: EUR 30-60 per person, 1 hour.
  • Small motorboat half-day (4 hours): EUR 150-250.
  • Catamaran full-day (8 hours): EUR 600-1,000.
  • Crewed yacht full-day (8 hours): EUR 1,200-2,500.

Season and local tips

The best season for boating in Amsterdam runs from April to October, with July and August as peak months. The prevailing wind is westerly, so plan your IJsselmeer crossings accordingly.

Tip: book early for July and August; boats can be reserved months in advance, and the weather is most reliable in late May and early June.

At a glance

  • Price band: EUR 110-2,500 per day
  • Peak months: July and August
  • Trip length: 1 hour to full-day
  • Capacity: 4-20 guests
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Amsterdam

Can I rent a boat for just an hour in Amsterdam?

Yes, many operators offer hourly shared tours starting from EUR 30 per person, ideal for a quick sightseeing trip.

What is the maximum group size for a private boat?

Private boats typically accommodate up to 12 guests on a motor yacht, while crewed vessels can handle 20 or more.

Is it safe to boat on Amsterdam canals at night?

Night boating is possible but requires extra caution due to low light and traffic; most rentals operate during daylight hours.

Can I fish from a rental boat in Amsterdam?

Fishing is allowed in some areas with a valid permit; confirm with the boat operator before departure.

BoatHire24 makes it simple to compare verified boats in Amsterdam, from small motorboats to luxury yachts. Whether you are renting for a family outing or a corporate trip, browse the listings to find your perfect vessel. Boat owners can also list their boats on the platform to reach a global audience. Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Amsterdam and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
